title: FDA Approves ICOTYDE for Plaque Psoriasis
text: Johnson & Johnson announced that the U.S. FDA approved ICOTYDE, also known as icotrokinra, for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis in adults and pediatric patients age 12 and older who meet weight and treatment criteria. ICOTYDE is described as the first targeted oral peptide that blocks the IL-23 receptor. This approval is strategically important because it expands J&J's immunology portfolio and provides an oral option in a market where many advanced treatments are injectable biologics. The approval was supported by a large Phase 3 program that evaluated adults and adolescents and included studies in areas such as scalp and genital psoriasis. For RAG explanations, this news supports the growth case for Johnson & Johnson by showing pipeline execution, product innovation, and expansion in a major immunology category.
